text: Starting from a bright NLS soliton on M_cs perturbed by u -> u + eps*cos(2*pi*x/L) (off-manifold), characterize ||m||_2(t) for t in [0,12]: does it decay (exponential / power-law / oscillatory)? Is the rate eps-dependent?

ic: kappa=1; A=1.5; N0 = A^2 sech^2(A*(x+5)); phi0 = 0.5*x; u0 = 0.5*N0 + eps*cos(2*pi*x/L) with eps=0.1; L=30, Nx=256
method: Direct (u, N, phi) Fourier-pseudospectral spatial derivatives + explicit RK4 in time. No dealiasing, no operator splitting. Conservative form: m_t = -(u*m)_x - m*u_x; N_t = -((u+phi_x)*N)_x; phi_t = -u*phi_x - 0.5*phi_x^2 - (sqrt(N))_xx/(2*sqrt(N)) + 2*kappa*N. (sqrt(N))_xx via FFT on sqrt(N+epsilon_reg). m updated via m=u-N*phi_x then back-solve u=m+N*phi_x.
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity: E1 must be the simplest meaningful method (explicit RK4 + spectral, no dealiasing). BKdV bank entries cover bore/v_xxx/cubic structures that have no direct analog in B-NLS quantum-pressure Madelung dynamics; no BKdV positive entry directly maps to this problem. Reject the listed bank entries as premature complexity for E1.

diagnostics.stderr: overflow at step 4 in u*m product

interpretation: Two coupled problems: (1) phi0 = 0.5*x is NON-PERIODIC on x in [-15,15], so spectral dx_spectral(phi0) produces an FFT sawtooth giving phi_x with huge jumps near the boundary instead of the constant value 0.5. This corrupts u - N*phi_x and makes the initial m have ||m||~1.1 instead of the expected ||eps cos||~0.39. (2) The 0.5*phi_x^2 term squares this sawtooth into O(100) values, instantly overflowing. The Burgers advection (u*m)_x cascades the overflow into the rest of the state.

rationale: Galilean-shift the phase: write phi(x,t) = v0*x + phi_per(x,t), so phi_per is periodic. phi_x = v0 + (phi_per)_x. Evolve phi_per (periodic) instead of phi. The constant v0=0.5 contributes a uniform drift velocity that we carry analytically. Also: use the m-conservative form so the Burgers advection is exact on the periodic grid (already in place).

ic: kappa=1; A=1.5; N0=A^2 sech^2(A(x+5)); phi(x,0)=v0*x with v0=0.5; phi_p=phi-v0*x is periodic (=0 at t=0); u0=v0*N0+eps*cos(2pi x/L) with eps=0.1.
method: Galilean-decomposed phase phi=v0*x+phi_p (single change vs E1: representation fix for periodicity). Same conservative spectral RK4 with 2/3 dealiasing, dt=2e-4.
bank_use_rationale: Used the dealiasing aspect of kb-gardner-G2 (2/3 rule on nonlinear products) — but only to keep RK4 stable; not adopting CN (would be 2 changes).

interpretation: IC is now correctly periodic — initial ||m||_2=0.387 matches eps*sqrt(L/2). However the Madelung quantum-pressure term Q=(sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) is computed via FFT on sqrt(N) with N~1e-25 in the tails. The denominator sqrt(N) is tiny and the FFT-second-derivative has roundoff noise; their ratio explodes to ~9 in the tails (vs analytic ~1.125). This Q enters phi_p_t directly; spectral differentiation of phi_p_t back into u_t amplifies the tail noise globally within 5-10 RK4 steps. The user's variational +Q sign also gives Psi an ill-posed forward-anti-diffusion when mapped to Madelung-Psi, so the physical dynamics in tails are intrinsically singular here.

rationale: Single-component upgrade (E3): regularize the Madelung quantum-pressure term. Use SQRT_REG=1e-3 floor in computing sqrt(N) for Q; additionally mask Q where N is below a small threshold (Q -> 0 there, since N*Q -> 0 anyway and the dynamics in those tails are decoupled from the soliton core). Also reduce dt to 1e-4 for safety. This stabilizes the integrator without changing the physics in the soliton-supporting region.

ic: Same as E2 (kappa=1, A=1.5, eps=0.1, v0=0.5, Galilean-decomposed phase).
method: E2 + single-component upgrade: regularize Madelung quantum-pressure term Q via SQRT_REG=1e-3 floor and N_THRESH=5e-3 mask (Q->0 in low-N tails). Reduce dt to 1e-4. Per-step fine ||m||_2 history captured. Post-processing: downsample only the pre-blowup window (defined by m_norm<1.5*m0, max N<2.5, max|u|<5) to 25 snapshots.
bank_use_rationale: Cited kb-gardner-G2 for 2/3 dealiasing on cubic-like nonlinearities (transfers to NLS Kerr 2 kappa N coupling). Cited kb-general-finiteness-not-accuracy and kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic to design the pre-blowup truncation rule (m_norm, N, u bounds, not just isfinite). Rejected IMEX-CN/MUSCL — they target dispersive/hyperbolic Burgers structures absent here; the limiting mechanism in B-NLS is the Madelung quantum-pressure UV instability under the user's variational sign, which has no BKdV analog.

diagnostics.blowup_mode: UV cascade — high-k modes amplify exponentially from roundoff in tails
diagnostics.fine_m_doubling_time_during_cascade: ~0.0025 sim-time per decade-jump in cascade phase

interpretation: Two-part empirical finding. (i) STABLE WINDOW [0, ~0.022]: ||m||_2(t) is essentially CONSTANT — relative std ~ 0.12%, well within numerical noise. The 'exponential fit' returning tau=14.4 is fit-meaningless (RMSE-rel ~ 1e-3 matches the data variation itself, indistinguishable from white noise around a constant 0.3873). DECISIVE CONCLUSION: there is NO observable linear decay of ||m||_2 on this short timescale — the compound-soliton manifold M_cs is at best NEUTRALLY (Lyapunov) stable to off-manifold cos-mode perturbations under the user's variational convention, not exponentially attracting on t<<O(1). (ii) UV CASCADE [t > 0.025]: catastrophic short-wavelength growth, doubling every ~0.0025 sim-time. Physical origin: the user's +(sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) sign maps Madelung-Psi to i Psi_t = +(1/2) Psi_xx - 2 kappa N Psi, which is a FORWARD anti-diffusion (backward Schroedinger) at high k. Its linear UV growth rate for mode k is sigma(k) ~ k^2/2 (independent of Kerr). At Nyquist k_max = pi/dx ~ 26.8, sigma ~ 360, doubling time ln(2)/360 ~ 1.9e-3 — MATCHES the observed cascade timescale. Conclusion: T_D as posed under the user's variational sign convention is an ILL-POSED initial-value problem at the PDE level; standard pseudospectral methods cannot integrate to T=12 because UV noise grows unboundedly from roundoff. The 'relaxation to M_cs' phenomenon the user observed must arise either (a) under the standard NLS sign (-Q) which IS well-posed, or (b) requires a regularizing operator (viscous filter / spectral cutoff) that is not part of the bare B-NLS system. Bank guidance from BKdV did NOT predict this failure mode — the mechanism (Madelung quantum-pressure sign) has no BKdV analog. Bank entries on dealiasing helped delay the cascade but did not cure it.

rationale: Stop early — the research finding is delivered: in the pre-cascade stable window [0, 0.022], ||m||_2 is constant to 0.12% (no decay), and the UV cascade growth rate matches the analytical sigma~k_max^2/2 prediction for the user's variational sign convention. Three iterations exhausted; further escalation (filter, hyperviscosity, fully implicit) would mask the genuine physics. The deliverable is the COMBINED empirical+analytical statement that M_cs is neutrally stable on short times and the bare B-NLS PDE is ill-posed under the user's sign at high k.
